name: SceneUtil
description: Move between scenes (levels, menus, maps): replace the current scene with another, or layer a second scene on top.

content.text: A **scene** is one self-contained space in your game — a level, a menu, a map, a lobby. `SceneUtil` is how you move the player from one scene to another: starting a level from the menu, advancing to the next level, or returning to the title screen.

`Load` switches to another scene. You wire the scene you want into an asset field in the editor, then load it. The second value, `additive`, decides whether the new scene **replaces** the current one or **adds on top** of it:

rows: Replaces the current scene — the normal way to change levels.
Loads the new scene **on top** of the current one, keeping both.

lines: // Drag the next scene onto this field in the editor.
// Replace this level with the next one.

content.text: For moving between levels or screens, use `Load` with `additive = false`. This cleanly swaps the whole scene, which is what you want almost every time.

By default, replacing a scene clears everything in it. If you have an object that must survive between scenes — a score manager, background music, a game controller — mark it as **persistent** in the editor. Persistent objects stay alive when you `Load` a new scene, so their data carries over.
Put long-lived managers (score, settings, music) on a persistent object so a level change with `Load(scene, false)` doesn't wipe them out.
Looking for objects **inside** the current scene (all coins, all enemies)? That lives on [UniObjectUtil](/docs/uniscript/en-US/uniobjectutil#block-030) — for example `FindUniObjectsByTag`.
